items.headline: RBI keeps repo rate unchanged at 5.25%, revises GDP and inflation forecasts
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 5.25% in its June 5, 2026, meeting, maintaining a "neutral" policy stance. The RBI lowered its GDP growth forecast for FY27 to 6.6% from 6.9% but upgraded its CPI inflation projection to 5.1% from 4.6%, citing risks from the Middle East conflict. Measures to attract capital flows were also introduced.

items.headline: Asian markets decline, Yen wobbles near 40-year low
items.summary: On June 25, Asian markets generally declined, led by technology stocks, with the Yen wobbling near a 40-year low.

items.headline: Easing crude oil prices provide positive sentiment for India
items.summary: Brent crude fell to around $72.67 a barrel. The decline in oil prices is seen as a macro positive, potentially improving inflation, fiscal, and current account dynamics for India, and offering the RBI greater policy flexibility.

items.headline: US markets higher on potential US-Iran deal
items.summary: US markets traded higher on June 15, following comments about a potential deal between the US and Iran.

items.headline: Harikanta Overseas, Antarctica, Desi Farms India scheduled for audited results
items.summary: Harikanta Overseas, Antarctica Ltd., and Desi Farms India Ltd. had scheduled audited results for June 26, 2026.

items.headline: Simply Wall St highlights Indian penny stocks with positive earnings and low P/E
items.summary: Simply Wall St highlighted Swasti Vinayaka Synthetics (BSE:510245), Shangar Décor (BSE:540259), and Swasti Vinayaka Art and Heritage (BSE:512257) as Indian penny stocks with positive earnings and low P/E ratios as of June 26, 2026.

items.headline: Mixed Q4 FY26 results for companies like Aequs, Edelweiss, MCX, D-Link
items.summary: For Q4 FY26, some companies like Aequs and Edelweiss Financial experienced profit contraction, while others like Multi Commodity Exchange of India Limited and D-Link (India) Ltd. reported significant revenue and profit growth.

items.headline: Nifty Auto index advances on softer metal prices, easing supply constraints, and improved demand
items.summary: In the trading sessions preceding the holiday, the Nifty Auto index advanced, driven by factors like softer metal prices, easing supply chain constraints, and improving retail demand.

items.headline: Nifty FMCG and Pharma sectors trade higher
items.summary: The Nifty FMCG and Pharma sectors also traded higher in mid-June.

items.headline: Lower crude oil prices expected to benefit multiple sectors
items.summary: Sectors that are generally expected to benefit from lower crude oil prices include airlines, paints, chemicals, tyres, plastics, consumer goods, logistics, transportation, and automobile companies.

items.summary: IT stocks have been under pressure.

items.headline: NLC India and Reliance Industries exploring lignite gasification JV
items.summary: NLC India and Reliance Industries are exploring a joint venture for an underground lignite gasification project in Gujarat.

items.summary: Craftsman Automation completed an equity placement of ₹2,000 crore, and a promoter is looking to divest a 2.01% stake.

items.headline: Upcoming dividend ex-dates and bonus issue record dates in early June
